list.title: Sessions
list.childPromoted: {count} child task session will be promoted to top-level.
list.childPromotedPlural: {count} child task sessions will be promoted to top-level.

page.confirmCloseTabDescription: The CLI process will be terminated and the row deleted.

empty.title: No session open
empty.hint: Pick a session from the list, or spawn a new one. Keyboard: {kbdN} new, {kbdW} close, {kbdRange} switch.

spawn.title: Spawn session
spawn.description: Start a CLI session under a registered provider.
spawn.noAccounts: No Claude accounts configured — the gateway will use the system ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
spawn.tokenMissingTooltip: No token set — set token in Providers panel first
spawn.multiAccountHint: Multiple accounts configured — pick one for this session.
spawn.bypassOnHint: This session will run with elevated autonomy.
spawn.bypassOffHint: Off — confirmations and prompts behave normally.

accountSwitcher.tooltip: Switch Claude account (restarts the CLI process)
accountSwitcher.confirmSwitch: Switching account will restart the Claude CLI process. In-progress conversation state inside the CLI will be lost. Continue?

fileBrowser.title: Choose working directory
fileBrowser.description: Browse the gateway host's filesystem and pick a folder.

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: Browse, search and edit memories agents have stored via the opendray-memory MCP server.

memoryWorkers.title: Memory workers
memoryWorkers.errorDescription: The /api/v1/memory/workers routes are new in M25 — the opendray binary may need a restart to mount them and run migration 0029.
memoryWorkers.intro: Each memory-system LLM touchpoint can be served independently by the local <1>summarizer</1> endpoint (LM Studio / OpenAI-compat) or by spawning a headless <3>Claude / Gemini agent</3> in <5>--print</5> mode. High-quality narrative tasks (gitactivity, transcript) benefit from agent workers; high-frequency tasks (gatekeeper) stay on the local endpoint by design.
memoryWorkers.agentWarning: Agent mode spawns a headless CLI per call. Latency rises from <1>~1s</1> (summarizer) to <3>~5-15s</3>; cost shifts from CPU to your Claude/Gemini quota.

gatekeeper.label: Gatekeeper
gatekeeper.description: Pre-write filter on every memory_store. High frequency (<500ms target) — summarizer-only.

cleaner.label: Cleaner librarian
cleaner.description: Periodic LLM librarian. Judges aged memories as keep / stale / duplicate.

gitactivity.label: Git activity summariser
gitactivity.description: git log → 2-3 paragraph narrative every 24h. Naturally fits an agent worker.

transcript.label: Session transcript summariser
transcript.description: Session-end "what did the agent do" summary. Naturally fits an agent worker.

cleanupInbox.emptyDescription: No pending cleanup decisions across any project. The LLM librarian either hasn't run yet for the eligible memories, or it found everything load-bearing.
cleanupInbox.title: Cleanup inbox
cleanupInbox.subtitle: Cross-project pending decisions from the LLM memory librarian. Approving stale → deletes, approving duplicate → merges, approving keep → freezes the entry from being re-judged for a while.
cleanupInbox.orphanTitle: Truncated scope_key (old mirror import). Not a navigable project.

picker.title: Pick a project
picker.subtitle: Project memory is scoped by working directory. Pick one to manage its goal, plan, journal, and cleanup queue.
picker.orphanTooltip: Looks like a truncated scope_key (old mirror import bug). May have no project docs.

project.noCwd: Pick a project to manage its memory.

editor.goalPlaceholder: What are we building? One paragraph. Read by every agent on spawn.
editor.planPlaceholder: Active plan — what we are doing right now and what is next. Updated as work progresses.

tech_stack.label: Tech stack & structure
tech_stack.empty: Run a Claude session in this project — scanner refreshes on every spawn.

recent_activity.label: Recent activity (git → LLM)
recent_activity.empty: The git activity summariser runs every 24h; check back after the next scheduler tick.

journal.empty: No journal entries yet. Each session-end appends one automatically.

inbox.emptyHint: Agents file proposals here via `project_goal_set` / `project_plan_set` MCP tools.
inbox.warning: Approve will REPLACE the current {label} entirely.
inbox.warningSuffix: Review the diff below; this isn't a merge.
inbox.confirmDialogDescription: The current {label} will be overwritten with the proposed content. This cannot be undone via this UI (you can manually edit it back).

cleanup.hint: The LLM librarian proposes keep / stale / duplicate verdicts for this project's memories. You approve before anything is deleted.
cleanup.empty: No pending decisions. Either nothing aged into eligibility or the last run found everything load-bearing.

reset.dialogDescription: Deletes all stored project context for this cwd. This cannot be undone.
reset.alwaysDeleted: Always deleted: goal, plan, proposals, journal, cleanup decisions.
reset.alsoDeleteScannerHint: Auto-rebuild on next spawn anyway — leaving unchecked is usually fine.
reset.alsoDeleteMemoriesHint: Long-term facts the agent stored (user preferences, project facts). Cannot be recovered.

memoryInspector.statusBody: This is the embedder the gateway is currently using for every <1>memory_search</1> / <3>memory_store</3> call. If this doesn't match the configuration above, you have unsaved changes — click Save then Restart server to apply.

search.placeholder: Semantic search query (Enter to run; empty = browse)

records.addTooltip: Manually create a memory in this scope
records.deleteAllTooltip: Delete every memory under this scope/scope_key
records.enterScopeKeyHint: Enter a scope key to browse memories.
records.noMemoriesInScope: No memories in this scope yet.

row.editPlaceholder: Memory text — Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to save · Esc to cancel

toasts.syncEmptyDescription: Already in sync, or no Claude memory dir for this cwd.

bulkDelete.title: Delete every memory in this scope?
bulkDelete.description: This is a single SQL operation — all memories under the specified scope are removed atomically. Memories that were ingested via the Claude mirror reappear on the next <1>Sync .md</1> run; everything else is gone for good.

addMem.title: Add memory
addMem.description: Manually create a memory. Agents create these automatically via the <1>memory_store</1> MCP tool — this form is for cases where the operator wants to seed a fact without going through an agent.
addMem.textPlaceholder: Plain prose. The embedder turns this into a vector at store time; agents will retrieve it via memory_search.

picker.buttonTooltip: Pick from saved scope keys or active sessions
picker.empty: No saved keys or active sessions for {scope}.

migrationBanner.subtitle: {summary} — current embedder is <1>{current}</1>. pgvector partitions its similarity index by embedder, so older entries are silent until reembedded.

reembed.title: Reembed memories
reembed.description: Recompute vectors for memories stored under a different embedder so they become searchable again.
reembed.explainer: Each memory's text gets re-sent to the current embedder; the new vector replaces the old one in place. ID, scope, scope_key, metadata and timestamps are preserved. Search results take effect immediately — no restart needed.

header.todayTooltip: Open or create today's daily note

outline.label: Outline
outline.empty: No headings in this note. Add <1>## Title</1> lines to populate the outline.

empty.title: No note selected
empty.hint: Pick a note from the tree on the left, jump straight to today's daily log, or create a fresh one. AI-written project docs live under <1>projects/</1>; your personal scratchpads under <3>personal/</3>.

picker.noMatch: No existing folder matches. Save anyway to use <1>{value}</1> (lazy-created on first write).

vaultSync.title: Vault sync
vaultSync.description: Commit, pull, and push the notes vault as a git repository. Authentication uses your gateway host's git credentials (SSH agent / credential helper).

init.title: Vault is not a git repo yet
init.body: Initialising will run <1>git init -b main</1> in your vault root and add a sane <3>.gitignore</3>. After that you can commit your notes and configure a remote (GitHub / Gitea / GitLab) for cross-machine sync.

action.pullTitleNoUpstream: Pulls origin's HEAD; sets up tracking implicitly
action.pushTitleNoUpstream: First push — will set upstream to origin/HEAD
action.noUpstream: No upstream tracking yet — first push will set it.

commit.title: Commit
commit.hint: Stages every change (<1>git add .</1>) then commits with this message. Empty message defaults to a timestamped subject.

remote.title: Remote (origin)
remote.empty: No remote set. Add an HTTPS or SSH URL (e.g. <1>git@github.com:you/notes.git</1> or <3>https://tea.linivek.online/you/notes.git</3>) to enable push / pull.

conflict.headline: Vault has a paused {kind} with unresolved conflicts
conflict.explainer: Pull, push and commit are blocked until the {kind} finishes. You can either <1>abort</1> (restore the working tree to its state before the {kind} — keeps your local commits, drops the remote ones) or <3>force reset to remote</3> (discard ALL local commits + uncommitted changes; vault becomes an exact mirror of origin).
conflict.forceResetTitle: git fetch && git reset --hard origin/<branch> && git clean -fd
conflict.forceResetConfirm:
DESTRUCTIVE: this will
  • abort the in-progress {kind}
  • run git fetch origin
  • reset --hard to origin/<branch>
  • clean -fd (drop untracked files)

Any local commits not pushed to origin AND any uncommitted edits will be PERMANENTLY LOST.

Continue?
conflict.abortedDescription: Working tree restored to pre-operation state.
conflict.resetDescription: Local changes discarded; vault matches remote.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.httpsTokenOk: Will use the token stored for <1>{host}</1> in Plugins → Git hosts. ✓
auth.httpsTokenMissing: HTTPS remote on <1>{host}</1> with no opendray token configured. Push / pull will likely fail for private repos until you add one.
auth.ssh: SSH remote on <1>{host}</1>. Auth uses the gateway host's <3>~/.ssh/</3> (ssh-agent, identity file, host config). Verify with <5>ssh -T git@{host}</5> from the host shell.

autoSync.title: Auto-sync
autoSync.runNowTooltip: Wake the sync loop now (skips the wait, then runs whichever steps are due)
autoSync.enabledTooltipNoRemote: Configure a remote first to enable auto-sync
autoSync.noRemoteHint: No remote — push/pull will be skipped.
autoSync.pullEveryHint: Only used when Pull is enabled.

syncBadge.initTooltip: Vault is not a git repo yet
syncBadge.conflictTooltip: Vault has unresolved conflicts — click to recover

activity.title: Activity
activity.subtitle: Per-call audit of API requests made by registered integrations. Includes both inbound calls (a third-party app calling opendray with its API key) and outbound proxied calls (admin → opendray proxy → integration). Calls made directly by this admin UI are not recorded.

empty.title: No API calls recorded yet
empty.description: When a third-party app calls opendray with its integration API key, every request is logged here.
empty.stepWithIntegrations: Use an existing integration's API key in your third-party app
empty.stepRegister: Register an integration in Integrations → New
empty.footnote: Calls you make from this admin UI are not logged — only integration-attributed traffic is recorded.

detail.noConfig: This provider has no user-configurable fields.

claudeAccounts.title: Claude accounts
claudeAccounts.importLocalTooltip: Scan ~/.claude-accounts/ on the gateway host and register any new directories. The button is gateway-host only — see the tutorial.
claudeAccounts.importedNothingToast: Nothing to import — accounts already in sync.
claudeAccounts.addingBodySuffix: opendray's filesystem watcher will register the new directory automatically, or click <1>Import local</1> to scan immediately.
claudeAccounts.empty: No Claude accounts yet. Run the shell command above on the gateway host, then click <1>Import local</1> to scan.

channels.title: Channels
channels.subtitle: Bidirectional messaging integrations. Outbound notifications are filtered by each channel's <1>notify_on</1>.

empty.title: No channels yet
empty.description: Bundled kinds: Telegram · Slack · Discord · Feishu · DingTalk · WeCom. Pick one and paste credentials, or use <1>bridge</1> for a custom platform via WebSocket.

card.setupTooltip: Show adapter connection details + sample code
card.testBridgeTooltip: Bridge channels cannot be tested from the admin — connect an adapter first

dialog.descriptionBridge: External adapter (Python/Node/...) connects via WebSocket and presents this token.
dialog.kindImmutable: (immutable — delete and recreate to change kind)
dialog.cooldownInvalid: Cooldown must be a non-negative number of seconds
dialog.snippetCapInvalid: Snippet cap must be a non-negative number

notifications.hintAll: Receiving every session event. Click a tag to opt out.
notifications.hintNone: No events selected — outbound notifications muted.
notifications.onceReplyHint: Replying with non-command text in this chat resets the suppression — opendray forwards your reply to the session's stdin and re-arms the notifier.
notifications.embedSnippetLabel: Embed the recent terminal screen in idle notifications
notifications.snippetExplainer: When enabled, the idle card includes a code-block snippet of what the user would see in the live web terminal — Claude TUI chrome (status spinner, "bypass permissions" hint, separator lines) is filtered out automatically.

modes.onceHint: Fire once when a session goes idle, then stay silent until either the session ends or you reply via this channel.
modes.cooldownHint: Suppress repeats for the same (session, event) within the chosen window.
modes.everyHint: No suppression. Use only for low-frequency channels.

snippetCaps.0: No cap — chunk into multiple messages (default)

bridge.nameHint: Human label for the adapter. Shown in the channels list.
bridge.tokenHint: Adapter authenticates by sending this in the WS register frame (or as <1>X-Bridge-Token</1> header).
bridge.capsHint: Empty = accept whatever the adapter declares. Selected = only allow these capabilities even if the adapter offers more.
bridge.afterCreate: After <1>Create</1>, the adapter setup dialog opens automatically with the WebSocket URL and copy-pasteable Python / Node / wscat starter code.

setup.title: Adapter setup — {name}
setup.description: Run an adapter (any language) that connects to opendray over WebSocket using these credentials. opendray will route session notifications and slash-command actions through it.
setup.authInfo: <1>Auth:</1> send the token as <3>X-Bridge-Token</3> header, <5>?token=</5> query param, or <7>Authorization: Bearer …</7>. The first WS frame must be <9>{frame}</9>. Full spec: <11>docs/bridge-protocol.md</11> in the repo.
setup.wscatInstall: Install: <1>npm i -g wscat</1>. Once connected, paste the JSON line shown above to register, then send further frames manually.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: External apps that consume opendray. Reverse-proxy through <1>/api/v1/proxy/<prefix>/…</1> and subscribe to events via the WS endpoint.

empty.title: No integrations yet
empty.description: Register an external app to give it a scoped API key. Its code stays out of this repo.

card.managedTooltip: opendray manages this integration. Editing or rotating its key would orphan running sessions whose mcp.json holds the previous bearer.
card.consumerTooltip: Consumer-only integration — no HTTP service to probe
card.consumerOnlyHint: Consumes opendray's API. No reverse proxy mounted.
card.managedReadOnly: read-only — opendray bakes its key into every spawn's mcp.json
card.managedReadOnlyTooltip: opendray manages this row. To reset: delete ~/.opendray/memory.key and restart, or delete this row directly via SQL — it'll be re-bootstrapped at next startup.
card.rotateConfirm: Rotate the API key for "{name}"? The current key will stop working immediately.

register_dialog.title: Register integration
register_dialog.description: Issues a one-time API key. Copy it before closing — opendray never displays the plaintext again.
register_dialog.modeHint: Leave the next two fields blank for a <1>consumer-only</1> integration (third-party app that calls opendray's API but doesn't expose its own service). Fill both for a <3>reverse-proxy</3> integration.
register_dialog.scopesIntro: Pick the API surface this integration is allowed to call. Each toggle maps to a Bearer-token claim — opendray rejects requests that touch endpoints outside the granted set.
register_dialog.errorBothOrNeither: base_url and route_prefix go together. Set both for a reverse-proxy integration, leave both blank for a consumer-only integration.

reveal.description: This is the only time the plaintext key will be shown. Copy it now and update every consumer app — the previous key (if any) no longer authenticates.
reveal.discardTooltip: Discard the new key (rotation already happened — old key is gone too)
reveal.discardConfirm: Discard the new key? Rotation has already invalidated the old key — discarding means you have NO working key for this integration until you rotate again.
reveal.updateHint: <1>Update every consumer app with this new key.</1> The previous key has been invalidated server-side and will return <3>401 unauthorized</3> on the next request.
reveal.acknowledge: I have copied the key and will update my consumer apps. I understand opendray will not display it again.

edit_dialog.title: Edit integration · {name}
edit_dialog.description: Change scopes, version, or base URL. Name and route prefix are immutable — delete + re-register if you need to change those.
edit_dialog.consumerHint: This is a consumer-only integration. Changing base URL here would also require a route prefix; do that with delete + re-register.
edit_dialog.scopesIntro: Trim or widen the API surface this integration's API key authorises. Live tokens are unaffected — the new scope set takes effect on the next request.
edit_dialog.errorModeSwitch: Switching between consumer-only and reverse-proxy mode requires deleting the integration and re-registering — name and route_prefix can't change in place.

proxy.emptyDescription: Register an integration first; the console proxies through /api/v1/proxy/{prefix}/* using the admin token.
proxy.historyEmpty: no past requests for this integration
proxy.extraHeadersLabel: Extra headers (one per line, Name: Value)
proxy.stubText: Send a request to see the upstream response.
proxy.stubInjects: opendray injects <1>X-Integration-ID</1> and strips your <3>Authorization</3> header.

plugins.title: Inspector plugins
plugins.subtitle: Configure data sources surfaced in the right-hand Inspector panel when a session is open. Each plugin is admin-only and shared across all sessions. Click a section header to collapse it.

mcp.title: MCP servers
mcp.description: Model Context Protocol servers injected into every spawn (claude / codex). Vault entries live at <1>~/.opendray/vault/mcp/<id>/mcp.json</1>; secrets (referenced as <3>${KEY}</3> in env / headers) come from the <5>MCP secrets</5> section below.
mcp.empty: No MCP servers yet. Add one to expose extra tools to your agent sessions.

editor.description: JSON shape: <1>command</1>+<3>args</3>+<5>env</5> for stdio (default), or <7>transport</7> +<9> url</9>+<11>headers</11> for sse / http. Reference secrets as <13>${API_KEY}</13> — they get substituted at spawn time from the secrets file.
editor.idHint: Lowercase / digits / dash / underscore. Becomes both the directory name and the default <1>name</1>.

mcpSecrets.title: MCP secrets
mcpSecrets.encryptedTooltip: AES-GCM encrypted on disk; key stored in OS keychain
mcpSecrets.plaintextTooltip: OS keychain unavailable — file is plaintext on disk. Check the gateway log.
mcpSecrets.description: Values referenced from <1>${KEY}</1> placeholders in any <3>mcp.json</3> get substituted at spawn time. <5>Saved values are never returned over the API</5> — you can overwrite or delete them but not read them back.
mcpSecrets.empty: No secrets stored. Add one to start referencing it as <1>${KEY}</1> in your MCP server configs.
mcpSecrets.deleteConfirm: Delete secret "{key}"? Any mcp.json that references ${'$'}{{key}} will fall back to the literal placeholder until you set a new value.

editor.addDescription: Stored encrypted on disk if the OS keychain is available. Reference it from any mcp.json env / headers / args / url with ${KEY}.
editor.editDescription: Enter the new value to overwrite. The previous value cannot be recovered.
editor.keyCollision: Already exists — use Edit instead, or pick a different name.
editor.valueHint: Hidden as you type. Saved value is never returned over the API.

skills.title: Agent skills
skills.description: Reusable capabilities injected into Claude sessions as a Tier 1 index — the agent loads full SKILL.md on demand via <1>opendray skill describe <id></1>. Built-ins ship in the binary but can be <3>customized</3> — your edits land at <5>~/.opendray/vault/skills/<id>/SKILL.md</5> and override the embedded version. Use Reset to revert.
skills.builtinTooltip: Embedded in the opendray binary — click Customize to override in your vault
skills.overridesBuiltinTooltip: This vault skill overrides the built-in version of the same id
skills.customizeTooltip: Open the SKILL.md and save changes as a vault override
skills.resetTooltip: Delete vault override and fall back to the built-in version
skills.resetConfirm: Reset "{id}" to the built-in version? This deletes your vault SKILL.md and falls back to the embedded copy.
skills.deleteConfirm: Delete skill "{id}" from your vault? This removes the SKILL.md file.

editor.customizeDescription: You're viewing a built-in skill embedded in opendray. Saving will create a vault override at the same id — your edits live under ~/.opendray/vault/skills/<id>/SKILL.md and shadow the built-in until you Reset.
editor.editDescription: SKILL.md format — frontmatter with name + description, then markdown instructions. The description appears in the agent's Tier 1 index.
editor.idHint: Lowercase / digits / dash / underscore. Becomes the directory name under <1>~/.opendray/vault/skills/<id>/</1>.

customTasks.title: Custom tasks
customTasks.description: Click-to-run shortcuts surfaced in the Tasks tab. Leave cwd blank for global tasks visible in every session, or pin to an absolute path to scope.

dialog.description: The command is sent verbatim into the session's terminal. Same as typing it at the prompt and pressing Enter.
dialog.descPlaceholder: Boots dev infra and tails logs
dialog.cwdHint: Blank = visible in every session. Otherwise the task only shows when the session's cwd matches this absolute path.

gitHosts.title: Git hosts
gitHosts.description: One token per host — used by the Git tab to fetch pull requests <1>and by the Notes vault sync</1> when its remote uses HTTPS to a private repo on the same host. GitHub.com, self-hosted GitHub Enterprise, Gitea, and GitLab are supported.
gitHosts.empty:
No git hosts configured.
Add one to enable the PR list in the inspector's Git tab.
gitHosts.deleteConfirm: Remove git host {host}? PR queries against this host will stop working.

dialog.description: Token is stored on the gateway. Used only for read-only API calls (list PRs, etc.).
dialog.tokenHint: GitHub: PAT with <1>repo</1> scope. Gitea: token with <3>read:repository</3>. GitLab: PAT with <5>read_api</5>.

backups.title: Backups
backups.subtitle: Encrypted PostgreSQL dumps written to a pluggable target. Configure schedules + retention, or trigger one-off backups for a quick safety net.

inventory.title: What's in a backup?
inventory.description: Each backup is a <1>pg_dump --format=custom</1> of every table below, plus <3>manifest.json</3> and (optionally) <5>config.toml</5>. Counts are live; the bundle captures whatever's there at backup time.

restart.title: Restart opendray to activate backups
restart.description: Your passphrase is saved. The gateway only loads it at startup, so the feature stays off until you bounce the process.

setup.title: Set up backups
setup.description: Choose a master passphrase. opendray uses it to encrypt every backup blob. <1>Lose it and your backups become unrecoverable</1>, so save it in a password manager (Vaultwarden, 1Password, …) before continuing.
setup.generateHint: Server generates a cryptographically random passphrase and shows it once. You must copy it before continuing — there is no recovery path.
setup.pasteHint: Recommended: 40+ characters from a password manager.

generated.title: Save this passphrase NOW
generated.description: This is shown <1>once</1>. It will not be retrievable from opendray or anywhere else. Copy it into a password manager before continuing.
generated.copyFailedToast: Copy failed — select and copy manually
generated.ack: I have saved this passphrase to my password manager

status.pgDumpHint: Backups can't run until pg_dump is on PATH (or its absolute path is set in <1>backup.pg_dump_path</1>). Install <3>postgresql-client</3> matching your server's major version and restart.

backupsTab.deleteConfirm: Delete backup {id}? The blob is removed from its target.
backupsTab.empty: No backups yet. Click "Backup now" above to take the first one.

restore.title: Restore from backup bundle
restore.cleanLabel: --clean --if-exists (drop existing schema first; required when restoring over a populated DB)
restore.auditNotePlaceholder: Reason for restore — appears in slog
restore.ownDbWarning: You're restoring into <1>opendray's own database</1>. With "--clean" enabled this drops every table and replays the backup verbatim — irreversible. Type <3>I understand</3> to proceed.

schedulesTab.description: Recurring backups. The scheduler polls every 30s and runs the oldest due schedule.
schedulesTab.empty: No schedules. Add one to take automatic recurring backups.

targetsTab.description: Storage destinations. v1 supports <1>local</1> (disk on the opendray host) and <3>smb</3> (any SMB / CIFS share, e.g. UNAS or Synology).
targetsTab.deleteConfirm: Delete target {id}? Schedules referencing it will block the delete.

targetEditor.title: New backup target
targetEditor.kindPicker: Where do you want to back up to?
targetEditor.enableImmediately: Enable immediately (otherwise saved as disabled — useful for "configure now, turn on later")

smb.shareHint: Top-level share name on the SMB server

s3.endpointHint: Host (no protocol). AWS: s3.amazonaws.com · R2: <accountid>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com · MinIO: minio.local:9000
s3.secretKeyHint: Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted; never echoed back

webdav.baseUrlHint: Full URL including any path. Examples: https://cloud.example.com/remote.php/dav/files/me/ (Nextcloud), https://nas.local:5006/ (Synology), https://dav.jianguoyun.com/dav/ (Jianguoyun / 坚果云)

sftp.passwordHint: Either password OR private key required. If both, password is treated as the key passphrase.
sftp.privateKeyHint: Paste contents of an OpenSSH/PEM private key (e.g. ~/.ssh/id_ed25519). Leave blank for password-only auth.
sftp.hostKeyHint: OpenSSH-style server public key (run `ssh-keyscan host` to obtain). Leave blank to disable pinning (NOT recommended outside LAN).
sftp.pathPrefixHint: Absolute or relative to user home (optional)

rclone.rcloneHint: Requires the <1>rclone</1> CLI installed on the opendray host. First configure your remote with <3>rclone config</3>, then use the remote name below. opendray invokes <5>rclone rcat / cat / lsd</5> under the hood.
rclone.remoteHint: Name from `rclone config` (no colon). Example: gdrive, onedrive, dropbox-personal, baidu-pan
rclone.binaryPathHint: Override `which rclone`. Empty uses PATH lookup.

font.title: Font size
font.description: Scales the entire interface. Persisted per browser.

changeCredentials.title: Change credentials
changeCredentials.description: Verify your current password, then pick new credentials. All other signed-in sessions will be revoked.
changeCredentials.errorTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.
changeCredentials.errorMismatch: New password and confirmation don't match.

system.title: System status
system.description: Live status from the gateway's /health endpoint.

about.title: About
about.description: opendray v2 — the multiplexer + integration gateway for AI agent CLIs. Source under Apache 2.0.

header.title: Ambient memory — auto-capture & inject
header.body: opendray polls every live agent session every 10 seconds, extracts durable facts via a configurable LLM, and dedups before storing them in the shared memory pool. Configure which LLM does the extraction (Provider), when extraction fires (Capture rule), and what — if anything — gets prepended to the agent's system prompt at spawn (Injection profile).

providers.title: Summarizer providers
providers.intro: At least one enabled provider is required for capture to actually fire. Local options (Ollama, LM Studio, Integration) keep your transcripts off external networks.

dialog.title: Add summarizer provider
dialog.integrationNote: Integration providers resolve their base URL from a registered integration. Configure that under Integrations first; advanced wiring (extra_config) is DB-only in this release.
dialog.apiKeyHint: Stored encrypted (AES-GCM with the backup master passphrase). Never echoed back; only the fingerprint is shown after save.

rules.title: Capture rules
rules.intro: Each rule says "when this trigger fires, summarize new transcript messages and store the durable facts." Per-session rules override the global default. v1 ships 4 trigger kinds.
rules.empty: No capture rules yet. Add one to enable auto-capture.

dialog.title: Add capture rule
dialog.dedupHint: Higher = stricter de-duplication. 0.85 is the recommended sweet spot.

profiles.title: Injection profiles
profiles.intro: At spawn time opendray prepends a markdown banner of recent project memories to the agent's system prompt — IF a profile is configured. Without a profile, the model still uses memory_search on demand.
profiles.empty: No injection profile. Memories are not auto-injected at spawn — model still uses memory_search.

dialog.title: Add injection profile
dialog.hint: One profile per session_id (or global default). Per-session profiles can be added later via API; UI currently only manages the global default.

cost.title: Token cost (all-time)
cost.intro: Per-provider summary aggregated from <1>memory_summarizer_calls</1>. Local providers (Ollama, LM Studio, Integration) are priced as $0 — operator owns hardware cost.
cost.empty: No enabled providers — no cost data.

noteEditor.emptyNote: Empty note. Switch to Source to start writing.

export.title: Export data
export.subtitle: Take a one-shot zip bundle of selected logical entities. Bundles are kept on the server for 24 hours, then automatically reaped.

form.memoriesHint: Cross-CLI persistent memory rows (text + scope + metadata). Embedding vectors are omitted; importer re-embeds.
form.customTasksHint: Operator-defined tasks shown in the Inspector's Tasks tab.

integrationOptions.metadataHint: ID, name, route prefix, scopes — no API key material.
integrationOptions.plaintextHint: v1 bcrypt-only: no recoverable plaintext exists. Manifest documents this; nothing leaks.

form.confirmWarning: Type <1>I understand</1> to confirm. opendray currently stores only bcrypt hashes — selecting plaintext does NOT export any plaintext (the feature is reserved for a future release that keeps plaintext caches).
form.footnote: Audit logs and session transcripts are out of scope — covered by /backups (operator dump) instead.

history.empty: No exports yet. Use the form above to create one.
history.title: History

import.intro: Replay an export bundle (zip) into the live database. Conflicts (matching id, or unique route_prefix for integrations) are <1>skipped</1> by default. Memories are tagged <3>embedder=imported_v1</3> and need a re-embed pass before search returns them; trigger re-embed under <5>Memory → Maintenance</5>. Integrations are imported with <7>enabled=false</7> and a non-bcrypt placeholder key — operator must rotate before use.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: API callers — recent activity & error rates

gitHosts.title: Git hosts
gitHosts.subtitle: PAT credentials for GitHub / GitLab / etc.

customTasks.title: Custom tasks
customTasks.subtitle: Slash commands shown in the session task picker

cleanupInbox.title: Cleanup inbox
cleanupInbox.subtitle: LLM-proposed deletions / merges across all projects

empty.subtitleAll: Tap the Spawn button to create one.
empty.subtitleFiltered: Try a different filter or pull to refresh.

action.deleteDescription: Remove the session and its history
action.deleteConfirm: Delete this session permanently? Its ring buffer and history will be gone.

dirPicker.empty:
No subfolders here.
Pick this folder, or create a new one.

tasks.insertCommandSubtitle: Pastes without return so you can edit

notes.draftHint:
# {project}

Thoughts, todos, context for the agent…
notes.personalHint: Personal scratchpad — auto-saves as you type. AI agents do not write here.
notes.projectDocsHint: Architecture / spec / decisions / plan / retros — typically authored or maintained by an agent.
notes.noProjectMapping: Could not resolve a project mapping for this session. Check that the gateway has a notes vault configured and that the session cwd is set.
notes.emptyProjectDocs: No project docs yet. Tap + to create one, or let an AI agent generate from a prompt.
notes.locationDialogHelp: Pin this session's cwd to a specific folder under your notes vault. Leave blank to reset.
notes.locationStoredHint: Stored in <vault>/.opendray-projects.json — git-syncs with the rest of the vault.
notes.pinnedHint: Pinned to {path}/ (overrides {defaultPath}). AI agents author docs here too.

spawnSheet.title: New session
spawnSheet.errorRequired: Provider and working directory are required
spawnSheet.cwdHelper: Absolute path on the gateway host.
spawnSheet.argsHelper: Whitespace-separated; blank uses the provider's defaults.

bypass.subtitleOn: This session will run with elevated autonomy.
bypass.subtitleOff: Off — confirmations and prompts behave normally.

noProviders.title: No providers configured
noProviders.message: The gateway has no CLI providers enabled. Configure one under Providers (web admin) or [providers] in config.toml, then tap Reload.

claudeAccount.label: Claude account
claudeAccount.helperMulti: Multiple accounts configured — pick one for this session.
claudeAccount.helperSingle: Pick a configured account or use the default (env / system).
claudeAccount.noneHint: No Claude accounts configured — the gateway will use the system ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Add accounts under Settings → Accounts on the web admin.
claudeAccount.errorHint: Could not load Claude accounts ({error}). The session will spawn with the gateway default.

mcp.title: MCP

editor.jsonHint: JSON config — name, transport: stdio, command, args…
editor.validateJsonObject: Body must be a JSON object
editor.idLockedHint: Locked in edit mode — delete + recreate to change.
editor.jsonSchemaHelp: Schema: transport must be stdio, http or sse. For stdio set command + args. For http/sse set url + headers. Use \$secret:KEY to reference vault secrets.
editor.idLabel: id (URL segment, lowercase alphanumeric / dash / underscore)

secret.keyInvalid: Key must match [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* — same rules as a shell env var.
secret.helpRules: Shell-env-var rules: starts with a letter or _, then letters / digits / _ only.
secret.replaceHint: Paste new value (the previous one is wiped)
secret.deleteBody: Removes the value from the encrypted vault. Any MCP server that references it will fail until restored.

popup.editConfigSubtitle: Full JSON editor — vault-backed servers only
popup.viewRawSubtitle: Read-only inspector for the server JSON

mcp.deleteServerBody: Removes the vault directory for {id}. Sessions that reference this server stop being able to spawn it.
mcp.emptyServers: No MCP servers registered. Tap "New server" to add one.
mcp.emptySecrets: No secrets stored. Add one to feed sensitive env / headers into MCP servers without putting them in the JSON.
mcp.noVaultFileYet: No vault file yet — added secrets create it.
mcp.tapToReplaceHint: Tap to replace · long-press / trash to delete

providers.title: Providers

accounts.deleteBody: Removes the account and its stored OAuth token. Sessions already using this account stay running but reauth will fail.
accounts.importSyncedSnack: Already in sync — gateway has no new accounts.
accounts.addHint: Adding a new account is gateway-host only.
accounts.addBody: The new directory shows up here automatically. See the docs for OAuth flow steps.
accounts.intro: Sessions spawned with the Claude provider pick from these accounts (or fall back to env).

providers.listEmptyBody: The gateway resolves providers from its plugin directory at startup. Check the logs if you expect one.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.apiKeySubtitleRegister: Hand this to the integration so it can authenticate against /api/v1/{routePrefix}/…
integrations.newApiKeySubtitle: Hand this to the integration. The previous key has just been invalidated.
integrations.deleteBody: Removes the registration and revokes the API key. In-flight requests using the old key will start failing.
integrations.rotateBody: Generates a new API key for {name} and immediately invalidates the old one.
integrations.noMatchingCalls: No matching calls in the log yet.

form.validateRequired: Name, base URL, and route prefix are required.
form.apiKeyWarn: You won't see this key again.

integrations.emptyState: Register from the web admin: Integrations → New.

memoryWorkers.title: Memory workers
memoryWorkers.intro: Each memory-system LLM touchpoint can be served independently by the local summarizer endpoint (LM Studio / OpenAI-compat) or by spawning a headless Claude / Gemini agent in --print mode. High-quality narrative tasks (gitactivity, transcript) benefit from agent workers; high-frequency tasks (gatekeeper) stay on the local endpoint by design.
memoryWorkers.errorDetail: The /api/v1/memory/workers routes are new in M25 — the opendray binary may need a restart to mount them and run migration 0029.
memoryWorkers.summarizerInfo: Uses the registry default summarizer provider. Pick a specific row on the web admin.
memoryWorkers.agentWarning: Agent mode spawns a headless CLI per call. Latency ~5-15s (vs ~1s summarizer); cost shifts from CPU to your Claude/Gemini quota.

gatekeeper.label: Gatekeeper
gatekeeper.description: Pre-write filter on every memory_store. High frequency (<500ms target) — summarizer-only.

cleaner.label: Cleaner librarian
cleaner.description: Periodic LLM librarian. Judges aged memories as keep / stale / duplicate.

gitactivity.label: Git activity summariser
gitactivity.description: git log → 2-3 paragraph narrative every 24h. Naturally fits an agent worker.

transcript.label: Session transcript summariser
transcript.description: Session-end 'what did the agent do' summary. Naturally fits an agent worker.

backups.title: Backups
backups.runConfirmBody: Triggers a fresh dump against the local target. The job runs server-side; this list will refresh as it progresses.
backups.deleteBody: Removes the blob from {target} and marks the row deleted in the index.

emptyNoTargets.headline: No backup targets configured
emptyNoTargets.body: Open the More menu → Targets to add a destination (local / S3 / SMB / SFTP / WebDAV / rclone). Then come back and tap "Run now".

emptyNoBackups.headline: No backups yet
emptyNoBackups.body: Tap "Run now" to take a fresh snapshot, or open Schedules to set up recurring runs.

backups.passphraseSaved: Your passphrase is saved. The gateway only loads it at startup, so changes only take effect after a restart.

wizard.title: Set up backups
wizard.intro: Choose a master passphrase. opendray uses it to encrypt every backup blob with AES-256-GCM. Lose the passphrase and you lose the data — there is no recovery.
wizard.generateHint: Server generates a cryptographically random passphrase, you copy it to a password manager, then commit.
wizard.helperRecommended: Recommended: 40+ chars from a password manager
wizard.saveNowBody: This is shown ONCE. It will not be retrievable from opendray afterwards.

backups.savedConfirmCheckbox: I have saved this passphrase to my password manager
backups.pgDumpMissing: pg_dump is not on PATH. Install postgresql-client and restart opendray.

backupSchedules.title: Backup schedules
backupSchedules.noTargets: No backup targets configured. Add one from the web admin or the Targets screen.
backupSchedules.deleteBody: Removes the recurring spec for target {targetId}. Existing backup blobs are not touched.
backupSchedules.emptyList:
No schedules yet.
Tap "New" to create one.
backupSchedules.enabledOn: Scheduler will run this on cadence.
backupSchedules.enabledOff: Paused — no automatic runs until re-enabled.

local.label: Local disk
local.description: Folder on the machine running opendray

smb.label: SMB share
smb.description: Windows shares + most home NAS appliances

s3.label: S3 / compatible
s3.description: Amazon S3 + S3-compatible buckets (MinIO, R2, B2)

rclone.label: rclone (any)
rclone.description: OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox via the rclone CLI

backupTargetEditor.idHelper: Lower-case letters, digits, dashes. Defaults to the next available slot.
backupTargetEditor.enabledOn: Scheduled and ad-hoc backups can target this.
backupTargetEditor.enabledOff: Server will refuse to write backups here.
backupTargetEditor.pathPrefixHintSshFolder: Absolute or relative to user home (optional)
backupTargetEditor.secretKeyHintEdit: Leave blank to keep current. Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted.
backupTargetEditor.secretKeyHintNew: Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted; never echoed back.
backupTargetEditor.sftpPasswordHintEdit: Leave blank to keep. If both password + private key are present, the private key wins.
backupTargetEditor.sftpPasswordHintNew: Either password OR private key. If both, password becomes a fallback only.
backupTargetEditor.privateKeyHintEdit: Leave blank to keep. Paste OpenSSH/PEM contents.
backupTargetEditor.privateKeyHintNew: Paste the contents of an OpenSSH/PEM private key. Multi-line input — keep the BEGIN/END markers.
backupTargetEditor.hostKeyHint: OpenSSH-style server public key. `ssh-keyscan <host>` to obtain. Blank = no pinning (NOT recommended outside LAN).
backupTargetEditor.rcloneNote: Requires the rclone CLI on the opendray host. First run `rclone config` once interactively to authenticate cloud accounts.

githosts.title: Git hosts

form.nameHelper: Display name shown in PR lists.
form.enabledHelper: Available to sessions for PR / remote lookups.
form.validateTokenRequired: Token is required when adding a host.

githosts.deleteBody: Removes the credential. Sessions trying to list PRs from {host} will fall back to the unauthenticated API.
githosts.emptyList:
No git hosts configured.

Add a credential so the gateway can list pull requests across your repos.

channels.title: Channels

notifications.title: Notification preferences
notifications.notifyOnEmpty: No events selected — outbound notifications muted.
notifications.snippetHelper: Embeds the recent terminal tail in each notification.

modes.onceDescription: Fire once when idle, stay silent until reply or end.
modes.cooldownDescription: Suppress repeats within the chosen window.
modes.everyDescription: No suppression — only for low-frequency channels.

channels.bridgeEmptyAdd: Add one from the web admin: Channels → New.
channels.deleteBody: Stops the channel and removes its configuration. In-flight notifications addressed to it will be dropped silently.

telegram.description: Bot via @BotFather. opendray long-polls getUpdates and sends via REST. Buttons + reply_to_message work natively.
telegram.botTokenHint: From @BotFather. Stored in channel config; admin-only API.

slack.description: Socket Mode — no public webhook needed. Requires a bot OAuth token (xoxb-) and an app-level token (xapp-) with connections:write.
slack.botTokenHint: OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token. Needs chat:write.
slack.appTokenHint: Settings → Basic Information → App-Level Tokens. Scope: connections:write.

discord.description: Bot via Discord Developer Portal with MESSAGE CONTENT INTENT enabled. Connects to Gateway WS — no public URL required.
discord.botTokenPlaceholder: Bot token from Discord Developer Portal
discord.botTokenHint: Application → Bot → Reset Token. Invite bot with send_messages + embed_links.

feishu.description: App-level credentials. Uses event subscription webhook for inbound. Public webhook URL is generated below — paste it into the Feishu dev console.
feishu.afterCreateHint: Open the webhook URL from the channel card and paste it into Feishu Open Platform → Event Subscriptions → Request URL.
feishu.verificationTokenHint: From Event Subscriptions → Verification Token. When set, opendray rejects webhooks with a different token.

dingtalk.description: Custom group robot. Outbound only. Group chat → Robots → Add → Sign mode → copy webhook + secret.
dingtalk.secretHint: When the robot is set to "Sign" security mode, copy the secret here. opendray adds the timestamp + sign params automatically.

wecom.description: Group robot webhook. Outbound only (text + markdown). Group settings → Group robots → Add → copy webhook URL.
wecom.webhookKeyHint: Or paste the whole webhook URL into the field below — either is enough.

wechat.description: Push to personal WeChat via WxPusher. Outbound-only — push services do not relay user replies. Each recipient subscribes once via QR code.
wechat.appTokenHint: WxPusher → 应用管理 → 创建应用 → 复制 App Token.
wechat.uidsHint: Either UIDs or topic IDs is required.
wechat.urlHint: When set, tapping the WeChat notification opens this page.

skills.title: Skills
skills.resetBody: Removes the vault override for {id}. Sessions will fall back to the built-in body.
skills.deleteBody: Removes {id} from the vault. Sessions that reference it will fail until restored.
skills.overrideBanner: Saving creates a vault override with the same id. Sessions will use this body instead of the built-in until you reset.
skills.idHelper: Lowercase letters / digits / dash. Locked once created.
skills.emptyList: No skills configured. The gateway ships with built-ins (planner, code-reviewer, etc.).

customTasks.title: Custom tasks
customTasks.descriptionHint: One-liner shown under the task name.
customTasks.deleteBody: Removes the task from the catalogue. Sessions that already inserted it stay unaffected.
customTasks.introBanner: Define your own slash commands. They appear in the session task picker alongside the built-ins.
customTasks.validateProjectCwd: Project-scoped tasks need an absolute cwd path
customTasks.nameHelper: Shown in the inspector's task picker.
customTasks.commandHelper: The text inserted into the session when picked. Can be a CLI command or a Claude slash command.
customTasks.globalScopeHint: Visible from every session, regardless of cwd.
customTasks.projectScopeHint: Visible only when a session's cwd matches the path below.
customTasks.cwdHelper: Absolute path. Sessions spawned with this exact cwd will see the task.

notesPage.title: Notes
notesPage.deleteBody: This is irreversible. Vault git sync will remove the file on the gateway host too.
notesPage.emptyVault: Vault is empty. Tap + to create your first note.

deleteAllConfirm.title: Delete every memory in this scope?

about.title: About
about.tagline:
opendray mobile — multi-CLI gateway control.
Source: github.com/Opendray/opendray_v2

gateway.serverSettingsSubtitle: Listen address, logging, vault, memory, storage paths…
gateway.liveLogsSubtitle: Tail the gateway log buffer — same source as the web admin

changeCredentials.title: Change credentials
changeCredentials.explanation: Verify your current password, then pick new credentials. All other signed-in sessions will be revoked.

serverSettings.title: Server settings
serverSettings.restartConfirmBody: The gateway will exec itself. The mobile app may briefly lose connection.
serverSettings.restartQueuedSnack: Restart requested. Pull-to-refresh in a moment.
serverSettings.restartHint: Most sections need a gateway restart to take effect. The restart button is in the AppBar.
serverSettings.savedNeedsRestart: Saved. Restart the gateway to apply.
serverSettings.changesNeedRestart: Changes to this section need a gateway restart.

sectionDescriptions.general: Listen address, operator account, token TTL.
sectionDescriptions.logging: Verbosity, format, and on-disk log path.
sectionDescriptions.mcpRegistry: Vault paths for MCP servers + secrets file.
sectionDescriptions.backup: Encrypted DB backups + admin data exports. Passphrase lives in the keyfile (Settings → Backups).
sectionDescriptions.storageClaude: Where Claude transcripts live on disk.

fields.adminUserHelper: Effective when no keyfile or env var is set. Otherwise see Settings → Account.
fields.adminPasswordHelper: Send blank to preserve. For ongoing rotations use Settings → Account (keyfile-backed, no restart).
fields.idleThresholdHelper: Quiet period before a session is flagged idle. Go duration.
fields.idleCheckHelper: How often the idle reaper runs.
fields.rootHelper: Parent of notes / skills / git_root sub-paths.
fields.backupEnabledHelper: Even with this on, the backup subsystem stays off until OPENDRAY_BACKUP_KEY or the keyfile is configured.
fields.pathHelper: Empty = resolve from PATH at startup.
fields.listenHelper: host:port the gateway binds to. Restart required.
fields.backendHelper: auto picks the best available; local needs ONNX.
fields.similarityHelper: 0.0–1.0; results under this are filtered out.
